items.title: A Near-Autonomous AI Chemist Improves Challenging Reactions in Medicinal Chemistry
items.summary: OpenAI's new research demonstrates an almost fully autonomous AI chemist that can optimize complex reactions crucial for medicinal chemistry, accelerating drug discovery.

items.title: LoopCoder-v2: Efficient Test-Time Computation Scaling with Single Loop
items.summary: LoopCoder-v2 introduces a novel method that processes test-time computations in a single loop, significantly enhancing efficiency for large language models.

items.title: Ling and Ring 2.6 Technical Report: Instant Agentic Intelligence at Trillion-Parameter Scale
items.summary: This technical report details Ling and Ring 2.6, showcasing efficient and instant agentic intelligence capabilities at an unprecedented trillion-parameter scale.

items.title: New Research Shows How AMIE, Google's Medical AI, Could Help Manage Health Conditions
items.summary: Google's AMIE, a medical AI, shows promise in new research for its ability to assist in managing various health conditions, potentially transforming patient care.

items.title: Zone of Proximal Policy Optimization: Teacher in Prompts, Not Gradients
items.summary: A new policy optimization method, Zone of Proximal Policy Optimization, guides agents through prompts rather than gradients, offering a novel approach to learning.

items.title: ACE-Ego-0: Unifying Egocentric Human and Robotic Data for VLA Pretraining
items.summary: ACE-Ego-0 presents a unified dataset and framework for pretraining Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models by combining egocentric human and robotic data.

items.title: GameCraft-Bench: Can Agents Build Playable Games End-to-End in a Real Game Engine?
items.summary: GameCraft-Bench introduces a benchmark to evaluate the capability of AI agents to autonomously build playable games from start to finish within a real game engine.

items.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B: An Open-Source Coding Model for the Claude Code Moment
items.summary: Nous Research has released NousCoder-14B, an open-source coding model positioned to compete with proprietary solutions like Claude Code, offering a powerful alternative to developers.

items.title: Introducing LifeSciBench: A New Benchmark for Life Sciences AI
items.summary: OpenAI introduces LifeSciBench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate and advance AI models specifically for applications within the life sciences domain.

items.title: LectūraAgents: A Multi-Agent Framework for Adaptive Personalized AI-Assisted Learning and Embodied Teaching
items.summary: LectūraAgents proposes a multi-agent AI framework designed to provide adaptive and personalized learning experiences, incorporating embodied teaching methodologies.

items.title: Learning from the Self-future: On-policy Self-distillation for dLLMs
items.summary: This paper introduces a novel on-policy self-distillation technique for distributed Large Language Models (dLLMs), enabling them to learn effectively from their own future states.

items.title: How I Use Premortems with Claude and Codex to Anticipate Project Failures
items.summary: A developer shares a practical approach to using AI models like Claude and Codex for conducting premortems, identifying potential project risks before they occur.

items.title: OPD-Evolver: Cultivating Holistic Agent Evolver via On-Policy Distillation
items.summary: OPD-Evolver presents a method for developing a holistic agent evolver by leveraging on-policy distillation to improve agent capabilities.

items.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck Says Enterprises Are Still Figuring Out Their AI ROI
items.summary: Tiffany Luck from NEA observes that enterprises are still in the early stages of understanding and quantifying the return on investment from their AI implementations.

items.title: My AI Agent Got Dumber Mid-Session: Measuring Context Window Before Blaming MCP
items.summary: A developer recounts an experience where their AI agent's performance degraded mid-session, highlighting the importance of understanding and measuring the context window.
